sections.label: Integrations
sections.title: Integrations
paragraphs: ICOS integrations define how the product connects trusted inputs, governed services, and future external systems without weakening the core architecture.
The integration layer is designed to extend capability while preserving continuity, permissions, and user control.
Each integration must serve the product system rather than fragment it into unrelated tools.

sections.label: Vault Integration
sections.title: Vault Integration
paragraphs: Vault integration connects ICOS with durable knowledge structures, preserved reflections, and governed records.
This allows profile memory and user-authored material to remain organized rather than scattered across disconnected surfaces.
The vault layer supports long-term continuity and review without reducing the product to simple storage.

sections.label: Model Integration
sections.title: Model Integration
paragraphs: Model integration allows ICOS to route interaction through suitable intelligence layers while preserving product-level governance.
The system can evolve across model providers, local models, and future dedicated infrastructure without changing the user-facing continuity logic.
This keeps ICOS flexible at the intelligence layer while maintaining a stable product identity.

sections.label: Identity Integration
sections.title: Identity Integration
paragraphs: Identity integration governs how profiles, access states, and public or private visibility settings connect across the product.
It protects the boundary between user-owned continuity and public discovery surfaces.
This makes identity a governed product system rather than a passive account layer.

sections.label: Publishing Integration
sections.title: Publishing Integration
paragraphs: Publishing integration connects selected profile or knowledge material to public-facing surfaces when permissions allow it.
The product can support discovery, presentation, and controlled exposure without making private continuity public by default.
This creates a clean bridge between internal reflection and external publication.
